tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P1] Add runtime-neutral session-stream Zod schemas to @dorkos/shared
tasks.description:
Create the runtime-neutral snapshot + event contract as Zod schemas under a NEW `/session-stream` subpath of `@dorkos/shared`. This is the foundation Design A & C depend on (ADR-0263). Everything else in this spec is built on these types.

FILES:
- CREATE `packages/shared/src/session-stream.ts` (the new schema module).
- EDIT `packages/shared/package.json` — add an `exports` entry for `./session-stream` mirroring the existing pattern (e.g. `"./session-stream": { "types": "./src/session-stream.ts", "default": "./dist/session-stream.js" }`). The exports map already has `./schemas`, `./transport`, `./types`, `./agent-runtime`, etc. — follow that exact shape.

SCHEMAS TO DEFINE (all `z.object`/`z.discriminatedUnion`, export both the schema const and the inferred `z.infer` type):

1. `SessionStatusSchema` → `SessionStatus`: server-held status projection. Fields: `contextUsage` (z.object with token counts; nullable), `cost` (number, nullable), `cacheStats` (nullable object), `model` (string, nullable), `permissionMode` (reuse the existing `PermissionMode` union from `@dorkos/shared/types`), `todoCounts` (z.object `{ total: number; completed: number; inProgress: number }`, nullable), `runningSubagentCount` (number, default 0), `lifecycle` (z.enum `['idle','streaming','blocked','error','interrupted']`). All numeric/usage fields nullable so a cold snapshot can carry `null` before the first turn.

2. `SessionEventSchema` → `SessionEvent`: a `z.discriminatedUnion('type', [...])` with a per-session monotonic `seq: z.number().int().nonnegative()` on EVERY member (assigned by the projector, NOT derived from JSONL line numbers — uniform across file-backed and log-backed runtimes per ADR-0263). Member `type` values: `text_delta`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`, `approval_required`, `question_prompt`, `elicitation_prompt`, `status_change`, `todo_update`, `subagent_update`, `turn_start`, `turn_end`. The three interaction members (`approval_required`/`question_prompt`/`elicitation_prompt`) MUST retain `remainingMs: z.number()` and `startedAt` (server-assigned — ADR-0262, preserved). Reuse `PendingInteractionDTO` field shapes from `@dorkos/shared/types` for interaction payloads. `status_change` carries a partial `SessionStatus`. Per-type payloads reuse existing DTO fields where they exist (do not invent parallel types for tool calls/results — reuse the StreamEvent payload shapes from `@dorkos/shared/types`).

3. `SessionSnapshotSchema` → `SessionSnapshot`: `{ messages: HistoryMessage[]; inProgressTurn: SessionEvent[] | null; status: SessionStatus; pendingInteractions: PendingInteractionDTO[]; cursor: number }`. `cursor` is the highest `seq` reflected in the snapshot. Import `HistoryMessage`/`PendingInteractionDTO` types from `@dorkos/shared/types`.

4. `SessionListEventSchema` → `SessionListEvent`: a `z.discriminatedUnion('type', [...])` with members `session_upserted` (carries a Session-shaped payload), `session_removed` (carries `{ sessionId }`), `session_status` (carries `{ sessionId, status: SessionStatus }`). Feeds the global status stream.

TSDoc REQUIRED on every exported schema and type (eslint-plugin-jsdoc enforced). Add a module-level `@module shared/session-stream` doc comment explaining this is the runtime-neutral contract owned by DorkOS, persistence pluggable per adapter (ADR-0263).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `packages/shared/src/session-stream.ts` exports `SessionStatusSchema`, `SessionEventSchema`, `SessionSnapshotSchema`, `SessionListEventSchema` plus their inferred types.
- [ ] `import { SessionEvent } from '@dorkos/shared/session-stream'` resolves (package.json exports wired; `pnpm typecheck` passes).
- [ ] Every `SessionEvent` union member has an integer non-negative `seq`. Interaction members keep `remainingMs` + `startedAt`.
- [ ] Unit tests in `packages/shared/src/__tests__/session-stream.test.ts`: valid snapshot/event parse; invalid `seq` (negative / non-integer) rejected; discriminated union rejects unknown `type`; `SessionSnapshot.cursor` accepts the max seq. Each test carries a purpose comment.
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run packages/shared/src/__tests__/session-stream.test.ts` green; `pnpm lint` clean for the new file.
tasks.activeForm: Adding runtime-neutral session-stream Zod schemas to @dorkos/shared

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P1] Extend AgentRuntime + Transport with getSessionSnapshot/subscribeSession/subscribeSessionList
tasks.description:
Add the three runtime-neutral contract methods to the `AgentRuntime` interface and surface them through `Transport` (Design A, ADR-0263). DorkOS owns the boundary; adapters own persistence.

FILES:
- EDIT `packages/shared/src/agent-runtime.ts` — the existing `getMessageHistory(projectDir, sessionId)` is at ~line 344 and is RETAINED (the Claude adapter will call it from `loadHistory()`); it is no longer the client-facing mechanism. Add the three new methods to the `AgentRuntime` interface, importing the contract types from `@dorkos/shared/session-stream` (created in task 1.1).
- EDIT `packages/shared/src/transport.ts` — add the matching methods to the `Transport` interface so both `HttpTransport` and `DirectTransport` (Obsidian) can implement them. HTTP maps `subscribeSession` to `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` (SSE) and `subscribeSessionList` to `GET /api/events` (SSE); Direct/Obsidian maps them to in-process async iteration (no SSE).

NEW INTERFACE METHODS (exact signatures — copy verbatim):
```typescript
/**
 * The authoritative current state of a session: completed messages, the
 * in-progress turn's events, status projection, pending interactions, and the
 * snapshot cursor (highest seq reflected). The adapter decides where messages
 * come from (Claude: loadHistory() from JSONL; stateless: the DorkOS EventLog).
 */
getSessionSnapshot(ctx: SessionOpts, sessionId: string): Promise<SessionSnapshot>;

/**
 * Normalized, monotonically-seq'd events for one session. The adapter maps its
 * native source (Claude: file-watch + the in-band SDK query; stub: its in-process
 * turn loop) into this stream. Pass sinceCursor to resume after a gap.
 */
subscribeSession(
  ctx: SessionOpts,
  sessionId: string,
  sinceCursor?: number
): AsyncIterable<SessionEvent>;

/**
 * Discovery + liveness across ALL sessions the adapter can observe, including
 * externally-driven ones (Claude adapter watches ~/.claude/projects; future
 * adapters expose their own). Feeds the global status stream.
 */
subscribeSessionList(ctx: SessionOpts): AsyncIterable<SessionListEvent>;
```
(Use the existing `SessionOpts` type already imported in agent-runtime.ts as the `ctx` shape — confirm the actual context type the interface uses; `SessionOpts` carries projectDir/cwd.)

IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: This task ONLY adds the interface methods and the `Transport` declarations + the HTTP/Direct transport STUBS that compile (throw `new Error('not implemented')` or delegate to a placeholder). The Claude adapter implementation is task 1.4; the test-mode/stub implementation is Phase 6. Adding the method to `AgentRuntime` will make `TestModeRuntime` (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/test-mode-runtime.ts) and any `FakeAgentRuntime` in `@dorkos/test-utils` fail to compile — add minimal compiling stubs to BOTH in this task (TestModeRuntime: return an empty snapshot / empty async generator; FakeAgentRuntime: add `vi.fn()` spies returning empty defaults) so the interface change is non-breaking. This intentional compile-break is the safety net the testing rule describes.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `AgentRuntime` interface has `getSessionSnapshot`, `subscribeSession`, `subscribeSessionList` with the signatures above and full TSDoc; `getMessageHistory` (~line 344) is unchanged.
- [ ] `Transport` interface declares the same three methods.
- [ ] `TestModeRuntime` and `FakeAgentRuntime` (`@dorkos/test-utils`) compile with minimal stubs; `pnpm typecheck` passes across all packages.
- [ ] HttpTransport + DirectTransport compile (stub bodies acceptable here; real wiring is Phase 2/3).
- [ ] `pnpm lint` clean (TSDoc on new exported members).
tasks.activeForm: Extending AgentRuntime and Transport with the contract methods

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P1] Build SessionStateProjector with EventLog + RingBuffer
tasks.description:
Create the server-side `SessionStateProjector` (Design B.1, ADR-0264): one instance per live session. It consumes an adapter's `subscribeSession`, assigns the per-session monotonic `seq`, maintains the live-state projection, appends to an in-process EventLog and a bounded RingBuffer, and absorbs the DOR-73 pending-interactions map.

FILES (server domain `services/session/` — currently only an `index.ts` barrel re-exporting Claude-Code session utilities; this adds the first true session-domain service):
- CREATE `apps/server/src/services/session/session-state-projector.ts`
- CREATE `apps/server/src/services/session/event-log.ts` (the in-process append-only EventLog: ordered `SessionEvent[]` per session, used as the completed-turn source for stateless runtimes and gap-replay overflow).
- CREATE `apps/server/src/services/session/ring-buffer.ts` (bounded ring of current-turn `SessionEvent`s; retained for a TTL after `turn_end` to absorb hard-refresh races). Cap ≤200 events/session (Performance Considerations), TTL ~10 min post-turn. Use named constants, not magic numbers (code-quality rule).
- EDIT `apps/server/src/services/session/index.ts` — export `SessionStateProjector`, `EventLog`, `RingBuffer`, and their types.
- CREATE tests under `apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/`.

PROJECTOR RESPONSIBILITIES:
1. `seq` assignment: a per-session counter starting at 0; each consumed event from the adapter gets `seq = ++counter`. The projector — NOT the adapter, NOT JSONL line numbers — owns `seq` (ADR-0263). Snapshot `cursor` = current counter value.
2. Live-state projection: maintain a `SessionStatus` (context usage, cost, cache, model, permission mode, todo counts, running-subagent count, lifecycle), the current `inProgressTurn: SessionEvent[]` (reset on `turn_start`, finalized on `turn_end`), the pending-interactions list, and todos/subagents. The DOR-73 `pendingInteractions` map (`apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/messaging/pending-interactions.ts`, `listPendingInteractions` selector with server-authoritative `remainingMs`/expiry) is ABSORBED into this projection — preserve the `remainingMs`/expiry selector exactly (exclude expired interactions, `remainingMs<=0`).
3. Append each event to EventLog (unbounded-but-trimmed; primarily for completed-turn history on stateless runtimes) and to RingBuffer (current turn only).
4. RingBuffer TTL eviction: after `turn_end`, retain the turn's events for the TTL, then evict; a timer or lazy-on-access sweep is acceptable (document the choice with a why-comment).
5. `getSnapshot()`: assemble `SessionSnapshot` from projection + (for the Claude adapter) `loadHistory()` completed messages or (for stateless) the EventLog. The projector exposes a `buildSnapshot(loadHistory: () => Promise<HistoryMessage[]>)` hook so the persistence source is injected, not hard-coded (ADR-0263 "own the boundary, not the bytes").
6. `replayFrom(sinceCursor)`: return RingBuffer/EventLog events with `seq > sinceCursor` for gap replay.

IDEMPOTENCY CONTRACT (must be unit-tested): events are applied idempotently downstream — `seq <= state.lastAppliedSeq` is a no-op. The projector guarantees strictly increasing `seq` so replay and live can overlap without dupes or gaps.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `SessionStateProjector` assigns strictly-increasing per-session `seq`; `cursor` equals the latest seq.
- [ ] Projector projects status (context/cost/cache/model/mode/todoCounts/subagentCount/lifecycle), pending interactions (expired excluded, `remainingMs` server-authoritative), todos, subagents.
- [ ] RingBuffer retains the current turn and evicts after TTL post-`turn_end`; cap enforced (≤200) with a named constant.
- [ ] `replayFrom(cursor)` returns only events with `seq > cursor`.
- [ ] DOR-73 pending map behavior preserved: `getSnapshot().pendingInteractions` matches the prior `listPendingInteractions` semantics (no expired, correct `remainingMs`).
- [ ] Tests in `services/session/__tests__/session-state-projector.test.ts` and `ring-buffer.test.ts`: seq monotonicity; ring retention + TTL eviction; replayFrom gap; expiry boundary. Each test has a purpose comment targeting a real failure mode (gap race, dup delivery, expiry boundary).
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/` green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Building SessionStateProjector with EventLog and RingBuffer

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P1] Implement the contract on the Claude Code adapter (JSONL-backed)
tasks.description:
Implement `getSessionSnapshot`/`subscribeSession`/`subscribeSessionList` on the Claude Code runtime adapter, mapping its native sources into the runtime-neutral contract (Design A, ADR-0263). This is the first REAL contract implementation; persistence stays JSONL-backed ("own the boundary, not the bytes").

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/claude-code-runtime.ts` — `getMessageHistory` is at ~line 357 (reads JSONL via `transcript-reader.ts`); keep it and call it from the new `loadHistory()` path. Add the three contract methods, delegating projection to `SessionStateProjector` (task 1.3).
- LEVERAGE `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sessions/session-broadcaster.ts` (file-watch on JSONL emitting `sync_update`) and `transcript-reader.ts` / `transcript-parser.ts`. Map file-watch deltas + the in-band SDK query the adapter already runs into normalized `SessionEvent`s fed to the projector.
- LEVERAGE `packages/mesh/src/discovery/unified-scanner.ts` (filesystem scan of `~/.claude/projects`) for `subscribeSessionList` — wrap its discovery into `session_upserted`/`session_removed`/`session_status` events, including externally-driven (Claude Code CLI) sessions. Add a debounced directory watch so external JSONL writes surface as `session_upserted` without client polling.

MAPPING DETAILS:
- `getSessionSnapshot(ctx, sessionId)`: call the projector's `buildSnapshot(loadHistory)` where `loadHistory = () => this.getMessageHistory(ctx.projectDir, sessionId)`. Completed messages come from JSONL; live state (in-progress turn, status, pendings) comes from the projector's in-memory projection.
- `subscribeSession(ctx, sessionId, sinceCursor?)`: yield the projector's live `SessionEvent` stream. The projector is fed by (a) the in-band SDK query events for turns DorkOS triggers and (b) `session-broadcaster` file-watch deltas for externally-appended JSONL. If `sinceCursor` is provided, yield `replayFrom(sinceCursor)` first, then live.
- `subscribeSession` normalization: translate Claude `StreamEvent`s into the new union (`text_delta`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`, the three interaction types preserving `remainingMs`/`startedAt`, `status_change`, `todo_update`, `subagent_update`, `turn_start`, `turn_end`).
- `subscribeSessionList(ctx)`: emit on lifecycle transitions only (not on a timer); debounce the file-watch (Performance Considerations).

SDK BOUNDARY: All `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` interaction stays inside `services/runtimes/claude-code/` (ESLint-enforced). Do not leak SDK types into the projector or shared schemas.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Claude adapter implements all three contract methods; `getMessageHistory` (~line 357) retained and called by `loadHistory()`.
- [ ] `getSessionSnapshot` returns completed JSONL messages + live projection + correct `cursor`.
- [ ] `subscribeSession` yields normalized seq'd events; `sinceCursor` replays the gap before live.
- [ ] `subscribeSessionList` emits `session_upserted` for a session created/updated OUTSIDE DorkOS (simulate an external JSONL write); debounced; no timer poll.
- [ ] Integration tests in `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/__tests__/`: snapshot assembly from a fixture JSONL; subscribe normalization; external-write discovery. Mirror the SDK-scenario builder pattern (`sdk-scenarios.ts`) where SDK-level events are needed; keep them inside the ESLint boundary.
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/server/...claude-code...` green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean; SDK-import ESLint rule not violated.
tasks.activeForm: Implementing the contract on the Claude Code adapter

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P2] Add durable GET /api/sessions/:id/events (snapshot to replay to live)
tasks.description:
Add the always-on durable per-session SSE endpoint that REPLACES the gated `GET /:id/stream` (Design B.3, ADR-0264). On connect it sends the snapshot then live; with `Last-Event-ID`/`?after=` it skips the snapshot and replays only the gap. This collapses DOR-73 Path A (pull) + Path B (re-emit) into one snapshot+replay mechanism.

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` — the gated `GET /:id/stream` is at ~line 567 (sends `sync_connected`, re-emits pending interactions on connect at ~line 589, heartbeat at ~line 612, file-watch with future-Last-Event-ID at ~line 618). ADD a new `GET /:id/events` handler; the existing SSE helpers `initSSEStream`/`sendSSEEvent`/`endSSEStream` are imported from `../services/core/stream-adapter.js` (line 3) and `SSE` constants from `../config/constants.js` (line 19). The pending-interactions pull endpoint is at ~line 166.
- USE the `SessionStateProjector` (task 1.3) via the active runtime obtained through `runtimeRegistry.getDefault()` and the adapter's `getSessionSnapshot`/`subscribeSession` (task 1.4).

HANDLER BEHAVIOR (`GET /api/sessions/:id/events`):
1. ALWAYS ON — no `enableCrossClientSync` gate, no toggle. Independent of any preference (ADR-0266).
2. Parse resume signal: `Last-Event-ID: <sid>-<seq>` header (the fetch-based `SSEConnection` already supports custom Last-Event-ID headers — `layers/shared/lib/transport/sse-connection.ts`) OR `?after=<cursor>` query param.
3. COLD connect (no resume signal): call `runtime.getSessionSnapshot(ctx, sessionId)`, emit it as a `snapshot` SSE event, then switch to live by iterating `runtime.subscribeSession(ctx, sessionId)`.
4. RESUME connect (resume signal present): SKIP the snapshot; replay from the RingBuffer/EventLog via `subscribeSession(ctx, sessionId, sinceCursor)` (which yields `replayFrom(cursor)` then live). Do NOT resend the snapshot.
5. Frame format: emit `id: <sid>-<seq>` before each `data:` frame (browser auto-sends it back as `Last-Event-ID` on reconnect). Send `: keepalive` every ~15s (`SSE.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS`). Set `X-Accel-Buffering: no` to defeat proxy buffering.
6. Expired pending interactions are excluded (projector already enforces `remainingMs<=0` exclusion).
7. Validate `:id` and `cwd` per the api.md route rules; 404 for unknown session via the existing pattern.

DEPRECATE `GET /:id/stream`: leave it functional ONLY until Phase 3 migrates the client off it, then it is removed (no lingering legacy per AGENTS.md "never tolerate deprecated patterns"). Add a code comment marking it deprecated-pending-client-migration with the spec reference. The client migration + removal happens in task 3.3.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` exists, always-on (no flag check).
- [ ] Cold connect: emits `snapshot` then live `SessionEvent` frames, each with `id: <sid>-<seq>`.
- [ ] Reconnect with `Last-Event-ID: <sid>-<seq>` replays ONLY events with seq > that value from the buffer and does NOT resend the snapshot.
- [ ] `?after=<cursor>` behaves identically to `Last-Event-ID`.
- [ ] Expired interactions excluded from snapshot + replay.
- [ ] `: keepalive` ~15s; `X-Accel-Buffering: no` header set.
- [ ] Integration tests in `apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/sessions-events.test.ts` using `collectSseEvents` / supertest + `FakeAgentRuntime`: cold connect sends snapshot then live; reconnect replays only the gap and not the snapshot; expired interactions excluded. Each test has a purpose comment ("core resumability guarantee").
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/sessions-events.test.ts` green.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the durable per-session SSE events endpoint

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P2] Decouple turn execution from the message POST (trigger-only, single delivery path)
tasks.description:
Make `POST /api/sessions/:id/messages` trigger-only so the turn's tokens flow through the projector to the durable stream as the SINGLE delivery path — no double-delivery (Design B.2, ADR-0264). This is a substantial server refactor; sequence it so the turn is never delivered twice (once in-band on the POST AND once on the durable stream).

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` — the current `POST /:id/messages` is at ~line 307. Today it: acquires the session lock (~line 335), calls `initSSEStream(res)` (~line 361), streams the runtime's `sendMessage` generator IN-BAND on the POST response (`sendSSEEvent(res, event)` at ~line 392), handles session-ID remap (~line 377), emits `done` (~line 388), and `endSSEStream(res)` (~line 405).
- USE the `SessionStateProjector` (task 1.3) and the Claude adapter's `subscribeSession` (task 1.4).

NEW POST BEHAVIOR (trigger-only):
1. Validate input (Zod, per api.md), acquire the lock as today.
2. Append the user message and START (or queue) the turn on the server: feed the runtime's `sendMessage` generator INTO the `SessionStateProjector` for this session (the projector assigns seq, buffers to RingBuffer, projects status). The turn now runs server-side, NOT bound to the POST response lifecycle.
3. Resolve the canonical session id (see task 5.1 for full DOR-74 resolution; here, ensure the POST RETURNS the canonical id in the response body).
4. Return quickly: `202 Accepted` + `{ sessionId: <canonicalId> }`. Do NOT stream tokens on this response anymore.
5. The turn's tokens are delivered ONLY via `GET /:id/events` (the durable stream from task 2.1) — the single delivery path.

MIGRATION SAFETY (the critical risk per ADR-0264 "migration must avoid double-delivery"): the sender's client (task 3.x) will, after this change, attach to `GET /:id/events` to see its own turn instead of reading it in-band. Sequence the client cutover (Phase 3) AFTER this server change but coordinate: during the transition window the durable stream is authoritative. Add a test that asserts a triggered turn is observed exactly once on the durable stream and NOT on the POST response.

SERVER-RESTART DEGRADATION (Non-Goal boundary, ADR-0262/0264): the in-progress turn's buffer is in-process; a server restart aborts the active turn (the runtime query/`canUseTool` promise cannot be resurrected). On restart the snapshot reflects completed state and the in-flight turn shows as `interrupted` (lifecycle enum from task 1.1) — NOT a frozen "Thinking…". Ensure the projector marks an abandoned turn `interrupted` on restart rather than leaving it `streaming`.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `POST /:id/messages` returns `202` + canonical `{ sessionId }` quickly; it no longer streams tokens in-band.
- [ ] The triggered turn is delivered exactly once, via `GET /:id/events` only (no double-delivery).
- [ ] Lock acquisition + validation behavior preserved.
- [ ] On server restart mid-turn, the snapshot marks the in-flight turn `interrupted` (no phantom live state).
- [ ] Integration test (`sessions-events.test.ts` or a new `sessions-trigger.test.ts`): trigger POST returns canonical id; turn observed only on the durable stream; restart-degradation path marks interrupted. Purpose comment: "migration safety / single delivery path".
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run` for the affected route tests green; `pnpm typecheck`/`pnpm lint` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Decoupling turn execution from the message POST

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P2] Wire global status stream events through subscribeSessionList
tasks.description:
Feed the existing unified `GET /api/events` multiplexed SSE stream with `agent_status`, `session_upserted`, `session_removed`, and `presence` events derived from the adapters' `subscribeSessionList()` (Design B.4, ADR-0265). The endpoint and fan-out infra ALREADY EXIST — this task connects the runtime contract to them.

EXISTING INFRA (confirmed):
- `apps/server/src/routes/events.ts` — `GET /` already opens the multiplexed SSE stream via `eventFanOut.addClient(res)`, sends a `connected` event, and heartbeats (`event: heartbeat`). Clients filter by SSE `event:` name.
- `apps/server/src/services/core/event-fan-out.ts` — `EventFanOut` singleton with `addClient(res)` and `broadcast(eventName, data)`; exported as `eventFanOut`.

FILES:
- CREATE a discovery wiring service (e.g. `apps/server/src/services/session/session-list-broadcaster.ts`) that, on server startup, iterates the active runtime's `subscribeSessionList(ctx)` (task 1.4 / Claude adapter) and calls `eventFanOut.broadcast('session_upserted'|'session_removed'|'session_status'|'agent_status'|'presence', data)`. Validate each payload against `SessionListEventSchema` (task 1.1) before broadcasting.
- EDIT `apps/server/src/index.ts` — start this broadcaster after the runtime is registered (near the existing relay→unified-SSE wiring at ~line 484). Ensure clean shutdown.
- Optionally add named SSE event constants so client + server agree on event names (avoid stringly-typed drift; code-quality rule).

BEHAVIOR:
- Emit only on lifecycle transitions, NOT on a timer (Performance Considerations).
- Server-discovered: externally-driven sessions (Claude Code CLI) surface via the Claude adapter's debounced directory watch (task 1.4) → `session_upserted` on `/api/events` with no client polling.
- This is ALWAYS ON (ADR-0265/0266); it is the primary mechanism replacing the client's 5s sessions poll (removed in task 4.1).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `GET /api/events` carries `session_upserted`/`session_removed`/`session_status`/`agent_status`/`presence` events sourced from `subscribeSessionList`.
- [ ] A session created/updated OUTSIDE DorkOS (simulate external JSONL write) produces a `session_upserted` on `/api/events` WITHOUT any client poll.
- [ ] Events emit on transitions only (no timer-driven spam); payloads validated against `SessionListEventSchema`.
- [ ] Broadcaster starts after runtime registration and shuts down cleanly.
- [ ] Integration test in `apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/` (extend or add `events-status.test.ts`): external-session write yields `session_upserted`/`agent_status` without polling. Purpose comment: "external-session liveness".
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run` for the events route tests green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Wiring the global status stream through subscribeSessionList

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P3] Build StreamManager module singleton + per-session Zustand store
tasks.description:
Create the client `StreamManager` module-level singleton that owns both SSE connections (durable per-session + global status) across route changes, React StrictMode double-mount, and session switches, and dispatches `SessionEvent`s into a per-session Zustand store read via `useSyncExternalStore` (Design D.1, draft StreamManager-singleton ADR; research `20260328_session_state_manager_architecture.md` + `20260327_sse_singleton_strictmode_hmr.md`). This removes the per-component stream lifecycle and the `initSession()`-resets-`currentParts` orphaning.

FILES:
- CREATE `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/stream-manager.ts` (FSD `shared` layer — it owns transport-level SSE singletons and must not import from entities/features). It uses the fetch-based `SSEConnection` already in `layers/shared/lib/transport/sse-connection.ts` (supports custom `Last-Event-ID` headers, backoff w/ jitter, heartbeat watchdog).
- CREATE the per-session store: `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/session-stream-store.ts` — a Zustand `Record<sessionId, SessionState>` where `SessionState` carries `{ messages, inProgressTurn, status, pendingInteractions, lastAppliedSeq, streamReadyCursor }`. NOTE: an existing `session-chat-store.ts` lives here and has `initSession()` that resets `currentParts` (the orphaning bug, ideation §3). Plan the migration so the new store is the source of truth and the old reset-on-switch behavior is removed; do not leave two parallel stores (AGENTS.md: no parallel patterns). Reconcile/replace `session-chat-store.ts` rather than adding alongside it.
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts` and `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/index.ts` barrels to export the new singleton/store (import from barrels only, per fsd-layers rule).

STREAMMANAGER REQUIREMENTS:
1. Module-level singleton, HMR-safe via `import.meta.hot.data` (research `20260327_sse_singleton_strictmode_hmr`) so HMR/StrictMode double-mount does not open duplicate connections.
2. Owns the durable session SSE for the ACTIVE session (`GET /api/sessions/:id/events`) and the global status SSE (`GET /api/events`). Two connections total per client (Design B.6 connection budget).
3. `attach(sessionId)` / `detach(sessionId)` API: switching sessions re-targets the durable connection without tearing down/recreating on StrictMode double-mount.
4. Idempotent apply by `seq`: when a `SessionEvent` arrives, if `event.seq <= state.lastAppliedSeq` it is a NO-OP; else apply and advance `lastAppliedSeq`. This makes replay+live overlap safe (no dupes/gaps) — the contract from task 1.1/1.3.
5. Dispatches into the Zustand `Record<sessionId, SessionState>`; React reads via `useSyncExternalStore` (or Zustand's `useStore` selector which uses it internally).
6. Global status events (`session_upserted`/`agent_status`/etc.) update a sessions/status slice the sidebar + status bar read (consumed by Phase 4 tasks).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `StreamManager` is a module singleton; HMR/StrictMode double-mount does not duplicate or drop connections.
- [ ] `attach`/`detach` re-targets the durable session connection on session switch.
- [ ] Apply is idempotent by `seq` (`seq <= lastAppliedSeq` is a no-op).
- [ ] Per-session Zustand `Record<sessionId, SessionState>` replaces the orphaning `initSession()`-reset behavior of `session-chat-store.ts`; no parallel duplicate store remains.
- [ ] Tests `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/__tests__/stream-manager.test.ts` + `entities/session/model/__tests__/session-stream-store.test.ts`: survives session switch + StrictMode double-mount without dropping/duplicating the connection; applies events idempotently by seq; dup event is a no-op. Use mock `Transport` via `TransportProvider`. Purpose comments target real failure modes.
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run` for these files green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean; no FSD layer violations (shared singleton imports nothing from entities/features).
tasks.activeForm: Building the StreamManager singleton and per-session store

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P3] Implement subscribe-first hydration; status items read from snapshot
tasks.description:
Wire the subscribe-first hydration protocol into the chat hooks so opening/refreshing/switching a session hydrates to accurate current state and continues streaming, and so status items populate immediately from the snapshot (Design D.2). Requirements #1, #2, #4.

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-history.ts` — the history `useQuery` is at ~line 91 (queryKey `['messages', sessionId, selectedCwd]`). Retain it ONLY as a snapshot source for completed turns where useful; the durable stream (via StreamManager, task 3.1) is now the live mechanism. The polling gate `if (!enableMessagePolling) return false;` is at ~line 99 (handled in task 4.2). The flag-gated sync SSE `syncUrl` is at ~line 156-162 (`if (!sessionId || isStreaming || !enableCrossClientSync) return null;`) — this gate and the whole `/api/sessions/:id/stream` subscription is REMOVED in task 3.3; coordinate.
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-chat-session.ts` — the top-level orchestrator; `initSession()` on mount/`sessionId` change resets `currentParts` (orphaning). Route hydration through the StreamManager + per-session store (task 3.1) instead.
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx` — currently computes context usage/cost/cache/model from in-memory store state that is `null` on cold load. Change it to read from `SessionState.status` (hydrated by the snapshot), so context usage etc. are populated immediately on refresh/switch.

HYDRATION PROTOCOL (subscribe-first, research §5 / ideation §5 "canonical pattern"):
1. On opening a session, StreamManager opens the durable SSE FIRST and records its `stream_ready` cursor.
2. Apply the `snapshot` the stream sends (messages + status + pendings + cursor) into the per-session store.
3. Replay the gap `[snapshotCursor, streamReadyCursor]` (the server handles this via Last-Event-ID/`?after=`); apply idempotently by `seq`.
4. Go live. Because apply is idempotent (`seq <= lastAppliedSeq` no-op), replay and live overlap safely — no gap, no dupes.
5. Status bar reads `SessionState.status` so context usage/cost/cache/model/permission-mode/todo-counts are non-null immediately on cold mount (requirement #4).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Opening/refreshing a session hydrates messages + status from the snapshot and continues streaming an in-progress turn (requirement #1).
- [ ] Switching sessions loads each session's state from the per-session store and continues streaming (requirement #2).
- [ ] `ChatStatusSection` shows correct context usage/model immediately on cold mount/refresh — not `null`-until-next-event (requirement #4).
- [ ] `currentParts` orphaning on switch is gone (state lives in the per-session store, not component-local reset).
- [ ] Tests `use-session-history.test.tsx` / `use-chat-session.test.tsx` (RTL + jsdom + mock `Transport`): snapshot populates messages + status (context usage non-null on cold mount); gap replay + live with no dupes. Purpose comments.
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run` for these files green; verify with `pnpm test -- --run` for the DEV-env gotcha (reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha); `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Implementing subscribe-first hydration and snapshot-backed status

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P3] Remove enableCrossClientSync flag + syncUrl gate; migrate client store; delete GET /:id/stream
tasks.description:
Make hydration + live sync always-on by REMOVING the `enableCrossClientSync` ("Multi-window sync") feature flag entirely (Design E, ADR-0266). This requires a client persisted-store migration and an AdvancedTab UI change, and finishes the deprecation of `GET /:id/stream`.

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store/app-store-helpers.ts` — `BOOL_KEYS` has `enableCrossClientSync: 'dorkos-enable-message-polling'`-style localStorage key entry and `BOOL_DEFAULTS` sets `enableCrossClientSync: false` at ~line 85 (and `enableMessagePolling: false` at line 86 — KEEP that one, retyped in task 4.2). REMOVE the `enableCrossClientSync` key from `BOOL_KEYS` and `BOOL_DEFAULTS`. The persisted localStorage key for it must be cleaned up — add a one-time store migration that deletes the orphaned `dorkos-enable-cross-client-sync` localStorage entry on load so removing the field doesn't leave dead persisted state (AGENTS.md: no lingering cruft). Follow the persisted-boolean migration pattern already used in this file/app-store.
- EDIT the app-store slice/types that expose `enableCrossClientSync` + `setEnableCrossClientSync` (grep the `app-store` dir) — remove the field, the setter, and any selector.
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-history.ts` — DELETE the `syncUrl` useMemo gate at ~line 156-162 (`if (!sessionId || isStreaming || !enableCrossClientSync) return null;`) and the entire flag-gated `/api/sessions/:id/stream` subscription, `sync_update`/`presence`/Path B re-emit handlers (~line 164+). Live sync is now the StreamManager durable stream (task 3.1/3.2). Remove the now-dead `enableCrossClientSync` prop threading through `use-chat-session.ts`.
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/AdvancedTab.tsx` — REMOVE the "Multi-window sync" `SwitchSettingRow` (~line 63-68) and its `enableCrossClientSync`/`setEnableCrossClientSync` store reads (~line 28-29). Keep the "Background refresh" row (re-described in task 4.2). Update the section copy at ~line 56-60 since multi-window sync is now automatic/always-on.
- DELETE `GET /api/sessions/:id/stream` in `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` (~line 567) now that no client references it (it was marked deprecated-pending-migration in task 2.1). Remove its now-orphaned helpers if unused. Update any server tests referencing `/stream`.

MIGRATION REQUIREMENT: removing a persisted preference requires a client store migration (ADR-0266 Negative consequence). The migration must (a) drop the field from defaults, (b) purge the stale localStorage key, (c) not throw if the key is absent. Add a test for the migration.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `enableCrossClientSync` is gone from `BOOL_KEYS`, `BOOL_DEFAULTS`, the app-store slice, selectors, and all prop threading; `pnpm typecheck` confirms no dangling references.
- [ ] One-time migration purges the stale localStorage key; absent-key case is a no-op (tested).
- [ ] The `syncUrl` gate and `/api/sessions/:id/stream` subscription are removed from `use-session-history.ts`; cross-client live sync still works (now via StreamManager) — verify a second window receives live updates by default.
- [ ] "Multi-window sync" toggle removed from `AdvancedTab.tsx`; section copy updated; "Background refresh" row retained.
- [ ] `GET /api/sessions/:id/stream` deleted server-side; no client or test references it.
- [ ] Tests: app-store migration test (`app-store-helpers` or store test); `use-session-history.test.tsx` updated for the removed gate; AdvancedTab test updated. `pnpm test -- --run` green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Removing the Multi-window-sync flag and deleting the legacy stream

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P4] Subscribe sidebar + status to global stream; remove the 5s sessions poll
tasks.description:
Make the sidebar agent/session list and status items accurate after refresh and live without timer polling by subscribing them to the store fed by the global `GET /api/events` stream (task 2.3 / 3.1) and REMOVING the 5s sessions poll (Design D.5, ADR-0265). Requirements #3, #4.

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-sessions.ts` — the sessions `useQuery` is at ~line 28 (queryKey `['sessions', selectedCwd]`) with `refetchInterval: QUERY_TIMING.SESSIONS_REFETCH_MS` at ~line 31 (the 5s poll) and `refetchIntervalInBackground: false`. REMOVE the `refetchInterval` poll. Keep the query as the COLD-LOAD source (initial list on mount), but drive live updates from the global status store slice (StreamManager, task 3.1): on `session_upserted`/`session_removed`/`session_status` events, update the sessions list (e.g. `queryClient.setQueryData(['sessions', cwd], ...)` or read directly from the store slice). The existing `setQueryData(['sessions', ...])` helper at ~line 16 shows the merge pattern to reuse.
- EDIT the sidebar consumers (`SessionsView`/`SessionRow`, referenced in ideation §3) only if needed to read live status; prefer feeding through `use-sessions` so UI components stay thin.
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-chat-status-sync.ts` and/or `ChatStatusSection.tsx` — ensure status items also reflect live `agent_status`/`session_status` from the global stream (in addition to the per-session snapshot from task 3.2).

BEHAVIOR:
- Sidebar list is accurate immediately after a hard refresh (the cold-load query) and live-updates from the global stream — including sessions driven OUTSIDE DorkOS (Claude Code CLI), surfaced by server-side discovery (task 2.3) with no visible poll lag (requirement #3).
- No `['sessions']` timer poll remains (it was the 5s drift source, ideation §3).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] The 5s `['sessions']` `refetchInterval` is removed from `use-sessions.ts`.
- [ ] Sidebar list is accurate after a hard refresh and updates live via `/api/events` events (incl. external/CLI sessions) without polling (requirement #3).
- [ ] Status items reflect live `agent_status`/`session_status` (requirement #4) in addition to snapshot hydration.
- [ ] Tests `use-sessions.test.tsx`: no timer poll; `session_upserted` event adds/updates a session in the list; `session_removed` removes it; external-session event surfaces without polling. Use mock `Transport` + a simulated global-stream event. Purpose comments.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` for these files green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Subscribing sidebar and status to the global stream and removing the poll

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P4] Retain + re-describe Background refresh as an opt-in external-session polling fallback
tasks.description:
Keep the `enableMessagePolling` ("Background refresh") toggle but DEFAULT OFF and RE-DESCRIBE it as an opt-in client-side polling fallback for sessions driven outside DorkOS, since server-side discovery (task 2.3) is now the primary mechanism (Design E, ADR-0266). It must NOT gate correctness.

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/AdvancedTab.tsx` — the "Background refresh" `SwitchSettingRow` is at ~line 70-75 with description "Check for new messages periodically, even when no one is responding". Replace the label/description with the new copy (below). Keep the `enableMessagePolling`/`setEnableMessagePolling` store reads (~line 30-31).
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store/app-store-helpers.ts` — `enableMessagePolling: false` at ~line 86 stays (default OFF). The localStorage key `dorkos-enable-message-polling` stays. No migration needed for this field (it is retained).
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-history.ts` — the polling gate `if (!enableMessagePolling) return false;` at ~line 99 stays as the OPT-IN fallback; it uses the existing adaptive interval (`QUERY_TIMING.ACTIVE_TAB_REFETCH_MS`/`BACKGROUND_TAB_REFETCH_MS`). No correctness path depends on it now (live updates come from the durable + global streams). Verify nothing else gated correctness on this flag.

NEW COPY (label + description on the SwitchSettingRow):
- Label: "Background refresh"
- Description (approx., from the spec §E): "Poll for updates to sessions running outside DorkOS (e.g. the Claude Code CLI). Off by default — DorkOS already detects external sessions automatically; enable this only if external activity isn't appearing promptly."
- Update the section-level copy (~line 56-60) so it frames Background refresh as a fallback, not a correctness switch (multi-window sync is now automatic).

FOLLOW THE adding-config-fields PATTERN: this is a client persisted-boolean re-description (not a server `conf` field), so no `conf` migration — but confirm via the `adding-config-fields` skill conventions that no server-side schema is involved. If any docs/copy reference the old behavior, update them (handled in Phase 6 too).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] "Background refresh" remains a toggle, defaults OFF (`enableMessagePolling: false`).
- [ ] New description matches the spec's external-session-fallback framing; section copy updated.
- [ ] The `enableMessagePolling` gate at `use-session-history.ts:~99` remains the only thing it controls (opt-in poll); no correctness path depends on it (verified by grep).
- [ ] AdvancedTab test asserts the new label/description and default-off behavior.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Re-describing Background refresh as an opt-in fallback

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P5] Resolve client UUID to canonical session id and rewrite the URL (DOR-74)
tasks.description:
Eliminate the dual-id hazard: resolve the client-generated UUID to the runtime's canonical session id at creation/first message (returned by the trigger POST, task 2.2) and rewrite the URL `?session=` to it with `router.replace` (no history entry) (Design B.5/D.4, ADR-0267, DOR-74).

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/server/src/routes/sessions.ts` — the trigger POST (`POST /:id/messages`, refactored in task 2.2) must resolve the client UUID to the runtime's canonical id at session creation/first message and RETURN it in the `202` body `{ sessionId: <canonicalId> }`. For the Claude adapter the canonical id is the JSONL filename / SDK session id; use the runtime's existing remap path (the old in-band code handled a session-ID remap at ~line 377 — reuse that resolution logic, now returned in the response instead of emitted mid-stream).
- EDIT `apps/client/src/router.tsx` — the `/session?session=` loader generates a client UUID when no param is present (the DOR-74 dual-id source, ideation §3). After the trigger POST returns the canonical id (client submit path), call `router.replace` to rewrite `?session=` to the canonical id (NO new history entry). The URL holds exactly one canonical id thereafter.
- EDIT the submit path (`apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-submit.ts`) to read the canonical id from the POST `202` response and trigger the URL rewrite + re-target the StreamManager (`attach(canonicalId)`, task 3.1) so the durable stream follows the canonical id.

REJECTED ALTERNATIVE (ADR-0267): do NOT alias both ids server-side — that leaves two valid ids indefinitely and forces every id-keyed path to remember to alias. Resolve-and-rewrite is the chosen approach.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Trigger POST returns the canonical session id in the `202` body.
- [ ] On first message of a new session, the client `router.replace`s `?session=` to the canonical id once, with no extra history entry.
- [ ] The URL holds exactly one canonical id thereafter; refresh/URL-entry hydrates against that id (supports requirement #1).
- [ ] The StreamManager re-targets to the canonical id after rewrite (durable stream follows).
- [ ] Tests: a client test asserting the rewrite occurs exactly once and is stable (`use-session-submit.test.tsx` or router test); a server test asserting the POST returns the canonical id. Purpose comment: "DOR-74 dual-id elimination".
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Resolving and rewriting the canonical session id in the URL

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P5] Scope the compose-next queue to its origin session and add key={sessionId} (DOR-81)
tasks.description:
Fix the wrong-session queued-message bug (DOR-81): move the compose-next queue out of component-local `useState` into the per-session store keyed by `sessionId`, pin the flush to the origin session, and remount session-scoped UI on `?session=` change (Design D.3).

ROOT CAUSE (spec Background + ideation §3): `ChatPanel` does NOT remount on `?session=` change (no `key={sessionId}`); the compose-next queue lives in component-local `useState` and an auto-flush effect fires on a phantom streaming→idle transition, delivering a queued message to the WRONG session.

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-message-queue.ts` (and/or `use-chat-queue.ts`) — move the queue state from component-local `useState` into the per-session Zustand store (task 3.1, `session-stream-store.ts`) keyed by `sessionId`. Each session owns its own queue.
- EDIT `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-session-submit.ts` — pin flush to the origin session: capture `queueSessionRef` at enqueue time and BAIL the flush if `sessionId !== queueSessionRef.current`. Reset `prevStatusRef` on session change so the phantom streaming→idle transition can't auto-flush into a switched-to session. Add a defense-in-depth assert in the submit path: never submit a queued message whose origin session id !== current session id.
- EDIT `ChatPanel` (find via `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx` or equivalent) — add `key={sessionId}` (or move session-scoped state behind a keyed boundary / the StreamManager decouple from task 3.1) so component-local session-scoped state cannot leak across a switch.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] The compose-next queue lives in the per-session store keyed by `sessionId`, not component-local `useState`.
- [ ] A queued message ALWAYS flushes to the session it was composed in; switching away-and-back or to another session never misdelivers it.
- [ ] `prevStatusRef` resets on session change; the phantom streaming→idle auto-flush cannot fire into the wrong session.
- [ ] `ChatPanel` (or equivalent) remounts/keys on `?session=` change so session-scoped state can't leak.
- [ ] Defense-in-depth assert in the submit path rejects a cross-session flush.
- [ ] Test `use-message-queue.test.tsx` / `use-session-submit.test.tsx`: compose-next queue is pinned to its origin session and never flushes to the switched-to session (DOR-81 regression). Purpose comment: "DOR-81 wrong-session queue flush".
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Scoping the compose-next queue to its origin session

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P6] Extend test-mode runtime into a stateless DorkOS-log-backed contract adapter
tasks.description:
Prove the contract is runtime-agnostic by extending the existing test-mode runtime into a STATELESS, DorkOS-log-backed adapter that stores NO native transcript: it implements `getSessionSnapshot`/`subscribeSession`/`subscribeSessionList` purely against the DorkOS-owned EventLog (task 1.3) — exercising the non-JSONL path end-to-end (Design F, ADR-0263 Decision 1 end-to-end).

FILES:
- EDIT `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/test-mode-runtime.ts` — `TestModeRuntime` currently returns empty defaults for the contract methods (the compiling stubs added in task 1.2: `getMessageHistory` returns `[]` at ~line 112, etc.). REPLACE those stubs with REAL implementations that:
  - `getSessionSnapshot(ctx, sessionId)`: build the snapshot from the DorkOS-owned `EventLog` (the server persists the events the projector already buffers) — NOT from any JSONL/native store. `messages` are reconstructed from the EventLog's completed `turn_start`/`text_delta`/`tool_*`/`turn_end` events; `status`/`pendingInteractions`/`cursor` from the projector.
  - `subscribeSession(ctx, sessionId, sinceCursor?)`: yield the projector's seq'd `SessionEvent`s from its in-process turn loop (the scenario store `scenarioStore.getScenario` at ~line 74 already drives `sendMessage`). If `sinceCursor`, replay from the EventLog first.
  - `subscribeSessionList(ctx)`: emit `session_upserted`/`session_status` from the DorkOS-tracked set of sessions (no filesystem watch — proving statelessness).
- The DorkOS-owned EventLog (task 1.3) is the persistence; the test-mode adapter holds NO native transcript. This is the explicit proof that the contract has no baked-in JSONL/file assumptions.
- Keep `DORKOS_TEST_RUNTIME=true` gating (index.ts only imports this when the env var is set — never in production).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `TestModeRuntime` implements all three contract methods against the DorkOS EventLog with NO native transcript store.
- [ ] `getSessionSnapshot` reconstructs messages from the EventLog; `subscribeSession` yields seq'd events + replays gaps from the log; `subscribeSessionList` emits without any filesystem watch.
- [ ] Server unit/integration test (`test-mode-runtime.test.ts`): the stub adapter drives the FULL snapshot/subscribe/list contract with no native store — snapshot, live subscribe, gap replay, list discovery all work. Purpose comment: "Decision 1 / runtime-agnosticism end-to-end".
- [ ] `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` and `GET /api/events` behave identically with the test-mode runtime as with the Claude adapter (same client/server code path — no runtime branching).
- [ ] `pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/__tests__/` green; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Extending test-mode runtime into a stateless log-backed adapter

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P6] Run + extend /chat:session-switch-test for refresh-resumability and DOR-77 checks
tasks.description:
Validate the four user requirements end-to-end in a live browser and update the acceptance harness. Run the `/chat:session-switch-test` matrix green — including the DOR-77 `perm:bypassPermissions` checks (#2-#5) — and ADD a new case: hard-refresh mid-turn continues streaming. Flip the prior known-regression notes.

FILES:
- EDIT `.claude/commands/chat/session-switch-test.md` — the harness defines checks #1-#6 (a checks table at ~line 147-156; check #6 "Permission prompts survive switching" is documented as expected-PASS post spec #254 at ~line 158; the refresh step is at ~line 124). ADD a new check row for "hard-refresh mid-turn continues streaming" (requirement #1) and UPDATE the harness to assert refresh-resumability: after a hard-refresh mid-turn, the in-flight response keeps streaming (not a frozen "Thinking…") and the status bar shows correct context usage immediately. REMOVE the prior known-regression notes that are now fixed (the "checks #2-#4 blocked if permission bug present" framing at ~line 22 — that bug is fixed; reframe for the new always-on streaming).
- Use the `browser-testing` / `browsertest` skill methodology to drive real interactions: two concurrently-working sessions, watch both JSONL transcripts, assert streaming/subagents/queued messages/todos/permission prompts survive switches AND survive a hard refresh.

CHECKS TO MAKE PASS (map to user requirements):
- #1 (requirement #1): URL-entry / hard-refresh mid-turn loads accurate current state and CONTINUES streaming the in-flight turn; status bar populated immediately.
- #2-#5 (DOR-77, `bypassPermissions`): subagents run, queued/compose-next messages drain to the CORRECT session (DOR-81), todos update, no leak across switch.
- #3 (requirement #2): in-UI navigation between sessions loads + continues streaming each.
- sidebar accuracy after refresh (requirement #3); status-item reload on refresh + switch (requirement #4).
- #6: permission prompts survive switch + refresh (already PASS per spec #254; must remain PASS).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `/chat:session-switch-test` run produces a findings report with PASS for all checks incl. the new hard-refresh-mid-turn-continues-streaming case and the DOR-77 #2-#5 checks.
- [ ] The harness markdown is updated: new refresh-resumability check added; stale known-regression notes removed/reframed.
- [ ] Evidence-based report saved under `test-results/session-switch-test/` (per the harness convention) linking prior reports and stating each prior FAIL now PASSES.
- [ ] Requirements #1-#4 all demonstrably pass in the live browser with BOTH the default permission mode and `bypassPermissions`.
- [ ] No console errors / no double-delivery / no wrong-session queue flush observed in the run.
tasks.activeForm: Running and extending the session-switch-test acceptance harness

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P6] Add cross-client / multi-window scenario tests
tasks.description:
Add automated cross-client and multi-window scenario tests proving always-on hydration + live sync work without any toggle (ADR-0266) and that two SSE connections per client suffice (Design B.6). These complement the browser acceptance (task 6.2) with deterministic, CI-runnable coverage.

FILES:
- ADD server integration tests (`apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/`) or client integration tests (`apps/client/src/...__tests__`) as appropriate, using `collectSseEvents` (`@dorkos/test-utils`) + supertest for server, and mock `Transport` + `TransportProvider` for client.

SCENARIOS TO COVER:
1. Two simulated clients attach to `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` for the SAME in-progress session; BOTH receive the snapshot then identical live frames (cross-client live sync, always-on, no flag).
2. A second client connecting MID-TURN gets snapshot+replay and ends in the same state as the first (gap-free reconciliation).
3. The global `GET /api/events` stream delivers `session_upserted` to all connected clients when a session is created (multi-window sidebar liveness).
4. Connection budget: a client holds exactly two SSE connections (active-session + global), leaving HTTP/1.1 slots for approve/deny POSTs (assert no third standing SSE is opened for liveness).
5. Idempotency under overlap: replay + live overlap applies each `seq` exactly once (no dupes, no gaps) — the core reconciliation guarantee.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Tests cover the five scenarios above; each has a purpose comment targeting a real failure mode (gap race, dup delivery, connection-budget regression, cross-client desync).
- [ ] Tests pass with BOTH the Claude adapter (or FakeAgentRuntime) and the stateless test-mode runtime (task 6.1) where the path is runtime-agnostic — asserting no runtime branching.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` green for the new files; `pnpm lint`/`pnpm typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Adding cross-client and multi-window scenario tests

tasks.subject: chat-stream-reconnection [P6] Update docs, API docs, AGENTS.md, and finalize ADRs
tasks.description:
Update documentation, API docs, and AGENTS.md to reflect the runtime-neutral snapshot/event contract, the durable + global streams, the removed/retained toggles, and finalize the draft ADRs (Documentation section).

FILES / ARTIFACTS:
1. API docs (`/api/docs`, OpenAPI registry — `apps/server/src/services/.../openapi-registry` or per-route registration): ADD entries for `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` (snapshot→replay→live; `Last-Event-ID`/`?after=`; `id: <sid>-<seq>` frames; always-on) and `GET /api/events` (multiplexed `agent_status`/`session_upserted`/`session_removed`/`presence`). DEPRECATE/REMOVE the `GET /api/sessions/:id/stream` entry (the route was deleted in task 3.3). Regenerate API docs if the repo has a generation step (cf. the recent DOR-73 commit "regenerate API docs for GET /pending-interactions").
2. New/updated `contributing/` guide on session-streaming architecture: explain snapshot + resumable delta, the runtime-neutral contract (`getSessionSnapshot`/`subscribeSession`/`subscribeSessionList`), the `SessionStateProjector`/EventLog/RingBuffer, the client `StreamManager`, and HOW a new runtime adapter implements the three contract methods (use the `writing-developer-guides` skill conventions; check `contributing/INDEX.md` and update it). Touch `contributing/architecture.md`, `contributing/state-management.md`, and `contributing/data-fetching.md` where they describe sessions/SSE/polling.
3. EDIT `AGENTS.md` — the "Sessions" section currently states "Sessions derive entirely from SDK JSONL files" and "There is no separate session store" and "Cross-client sync via persistent SSE (GET /api/sessions/:id/stream)". UPDATE: DorkOS now owns a runtime-neutral snapshot/event contract with pluggable per-adapter persistence; live streaming is the always-on durable `GET /api/sessions/:id/events`; the legacy `/stream` endpoint is removed; cross-client sync is always-on (no Multi-window-sync flag).
4. EDIT external docs (`docs/` MDX, if any reference the toggles): document removal of "Multi-window sync" and the re-described "Background refresh" fallback copy.
5. Promote the five draft ADRs (0263-0267) from `draft` to the appropriate accepted/implemented status using the `adr:review` or `writing-adrs` conventions, now that the spec is implemented. Update `decisions/manifest.json` accordingly. Note ADR-0262 is EXTENDED/GENERALIZED (Path A/B collapsed into snapshot+replay) and ADR-0117 is extended.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] `/api/docs` documents `GET /api/sessions/:id/events` and `GET /api/events`; the `/stream` entry is removed; docs regenerated if applicable.
- [ ] A `contributing/` session-streaming guide exists (or an existing guide is updated) covering the contract, projector/buffers, StreamManager, and the new-adapter recipe; `contributing/INDEX.md` updated.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` "Sessions" section no longer claims JSONL-only derivation; reflects the contract + always-on streaming + removed `/stream`.
- [ ] Toggle copy documented (Multi-window sync removed; Background refresh re-described).
- [ ] ADRs 0263-0267 promoted from draft with `decisions/manifest.json` updated; ADR-0262/0117 noted as extended.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` (markdown/prettier) clean; `docs:reconcile`/`docs:status` show no new drift for the touched guides.
tasks.activeForm: Updating docs, API docs, AGENTS.md, and finalizing ADRs
